description: A segment of customers who consistently view and purchase from the same categories, with high add-to-cart rates, frequent purchases, and broad category diversity. They have not engaged recently but maintain high purchase conversion rates.
evidence: Top view categories: Books: 30.2%; Beauty: 25.1%; Toys: 15.2%
Top purchase categories: Books: 30.2%; Beauty: 25.1%; Toys: 15.2%
cautions: Segment has low silhouette scores, indicating behavioral overlap with other groups
No recent activity (last session: 210.5 days ago, last order: 438.1 days ago)
Category ratios are not dominant (view: 31.3%, purchase: 53.0%)

description: A segment of customers who frequently browse and add items to their cart but do not complete purchases, with a high average number of page views and a non-zero add-to-cart rate, but no recorded purchases or orders. They have not made a purchase in the available data and their last session was over a year ago. Their top view categories are Books, Beauty, and Toys, with a dominant view category ratio of 39.9%.
evidence: Average days since last order: none
Average dominant purchase category ratio: none
Top view categories: Books: 20.2%; Beauty: 19.0%; Toys: 13.8%
cautions: This segment was produced by KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores, indicating it is a behavior-based approximation, not a sharply separated natural group.
Avoid overconfident or marketing-style labels for this segment.

description: A small group of customers who engage deeply with the site (high page views and add-to-cart rates) but place orders infrequently (low purchase per session and high days since last order). They predominantly purchase in a single category (high dominant purchase ratio) and have not shopped recently. Despite high engagement, their spending is moderate.
cautions: Segment was identified via KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores, indicating approximate behavioral grouping
Does not account for unobserved factors like marketing exposure or device usage patterns
Purchase category diversity (2.15) suggests limited but not exclusive category focus
Top view/purchase categories share similar composition but no single dominant category exceeds 50%

segment_name: Frequent-Viewing Purchasers with Narrow Category Focus
description: A segment of customers who view many pages, add items to cart at a moderate rate, and exclusively make purchases. They have a high average order count, low purchase frequency, and spend modestly. Their activity is concentrated in a small number of categories, with over 80% of purchases in a single dominant category. All sessions result in purchases, and their view and purchase categories match exactly. This group has not ordered in over two years, though they had a session within the last year.
evidence: Top view categories: Books: 26.2%; Beauty: 21.7%; Toys: 15.6%
Top purchase categories: Books: 26.2%; Beauty: 21.7%; Toys: 15.6%
cautions: This segment was produced by KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores, indicating it is a behavior-based approximation, not a sharply separated natural group
Avoid overconfident or marketing-style labels as the segment boundaries are not precise

description: A large segment of customers with high engagement (page views, add-to-carts), universal purchase history, moderate order frequency, and log-scale spending, but no overlap between viewed and purchased categories. Dominant categories differ significantly between browsing and purchasing behavior, with extended inactivity since last order.
evidence: Top view categories: Books (18.6%), Toys (17.2%), Beauty (15.8%)
Top purchase categories: Beauty (24.2%), Books (23.5%), Toys (14.1%)
cautions: This segment was produced by KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores, indicating behavior-based approximation rather than distinct natural grouping.
Marketing labels or demographic inferences (income, age, occupation, etc.) were deliberately avoided as they are not observable in the data.

segment_name: Low-Frequency Purchasers with Narrow Category Focus
description: A large segment of customers who purchase exclusively but infrequently, with high category specialization in their purchases despite broader browsing behavior. They exhibit low purchase-to-session ratios and long gaps between orders, with no overlap between their most-viewed and most-purchased categories.
evidence: Top view categories: Books: 19.2%; Beauty: 15.9%; Toys: 15.0%
Top purchase categories: Books: 21.8%; Beauty: 20.1%; Toys: 15.9%
cautions: KMeans clustering produced generally low silhouette scores, indicating this segment may not represent a sharply defined behavioral group
Log-scale spend value (4.53) cannot be directly interpreted as monetary amount
No session frequency data provided to assess browsing behavior timing
Add-to-cart count (6.23) and page views (25.99) are not directly tied to purchase outcomes in this description
